help_page.title: Help & Guide
help_page.intro: How the app works, and the things that aren't obvious until someone tells you.
help_page.no_results: Nothing matches that. Try a different word — or the name of the screen you're on.

topics.heading: Drafts vs confirmed orders
topics.body: A draft is a request, not a sale. Nothing leaves your stock and no money is recorded until someone confirms it. That's why orders from WhatsApp arrive as drafts — the shop decides what's real.
topics.tip: If stock looks wrong, check whether an order is still sitting in Drafts. It hasn't been deducted yet.
topics.keywords: draft confirm promote stock deduct pending

topics.heading: The order board
topics.body: Created → In progress → On the way or In the shop → Completed. Each column is a queue: the order that has been waiting longest sits at the top, and every card shows how long it has been in its current column.
topics.tip: The clock restarts at each move, so it answers "how long has this been stuck here", not "how old is this order".
topics.keywords: board kanban columns status queue waiting timer

topics.heading: Late orders find you
topics.body: Once an order sits in one status longer than your alert threshold, its clock turns amber and it appears in the Late orders table on the dashboard — with which status it's stuck in and for how long.
topics.tip: Set the threshold in Settings. It's the same number the board and the dashboard both use, so there's only one to tune.
topics.keywords: late alert threshold overdue amber dashboard waiting

topics.heading: Weight, not pieces
topics.body: Everything is priced per kilo and stored to three decimal places. 1.250 kg at 87.50/kg is 109.38 — the app does that arithmetic so nobody does it on a receipt pad.
topics.keywords: kg weight kilo price decimals precision

topics.heading: You're signed out when idle
topics.body: After a period of no activity you'll see a countdown with "I'm still here". Ignore it and the app signs you out — shop terminals get walked away from. Anything you're actively doing keeps the session alive indefinitely.
topics.tip: Sign out at the end of a shift anyway. The next person should be working under their own name, or the order history credits the wrong person.
topics.keywords: idle timeout logout session security shift terminal

topics.heading: Cash position and revenue are different numbers
topics.body: Revenue is what you sold. Cash position is what's in the drawer — sales, plus money you put in, minus money you took out. They are never merged, because a supplier paid in cash changes one and not the other.
topics.tip: If those two figures agreeing feels like it should mean something, it doesn't. Only cash-in matching your drawer count does.
topics.keywords: cash revenue drawer position ledger reconcile difference

topics.heading: Cash sales log themselves
topics.body: Confirm an order paid in cash and a matching entry appears in the ledger automatically, tagged with where the order came from. You only enter money by hand for things that aren't sales: a supplier payment, a float, a correction.
topics.keywords: cash automatic ledger sale entry supplier float

topics.heading: Finding an old entry
topics.body: The ledger has search and date filters. Search matches the category, the note and the amount — and it matches what's on screen in your language, not just what's stored underneath.
topics.tip: While a filter is active the totals above the table show the filtered figures, so you can total one supplier or one day without doing sums.
topics.keywords: search filter ledger find date range totals

topics.heading: Closing the day
topics.body: Closing takes a permanent snapshot of the day's orders and cash, then resets order numbering so tomorrow starts at #1. It does not delete or alter a single ledger entry — every past entry stays visible.
topics.tip: It's a deliberate action, not a midnight timer, because shops don't all close at the same hour or every day.
topics.keywords: close day closing snapshot reset numbering daily

topics.heading: Editing stock needs a reason
topics.body: Changing a product's quantity directly asks you why, and records it. Waste, a delivery, a recount — all of it is kept, so "where did 4 kilos go" has an answer instead of an argument.
topics.keywords: stock adjust reason audit waste recount delivery history

topics.heading: Low-stock alerts
topics.body: The shop has a default threshold, and any product can override it. Lamb might need warning at 5 kg while a slow-moving cut doesn't. The dashboard counts anything under its own threshold.
topics.keywords: low stock threshold alert warning default product

topics.heading: Breaking down a carcass
topics.body: Pick a template — calf, sheep, goat — enter the actual weight of each cut, and the resulting stock lands in inventory. The yield against the expected breakdown is recorded, so you can see which supplier's animals come up short.
topics.tip: The recorded yield is the point. One carcass tells you nothing; twenty tell you who to buy from.
topics.keywords: dismantle carcass template cuts yield calf sheep goat supplier

topics.heading: WhatsApp orders
topics.body: A customer's message is read for lines like "2kg beef", matched against your products, and turned into a draft. Arabic and English both work, and Arabic-Indic digits (٢) are understood the same as 2. The customer gets an automatic acknowledgement.
topics.tip: The original message is kept on the order. When the wording is ambiguous, read what they actually wrote rather than trusting the parse.
topics.keywords: whatsapp message parse automatic draft arabic digits

topics.heading: Items it couldn't match
topics.body: If someone asks for "2kg beef and some liver" and you have no product called liver, the draft shows an amber box listing what wasn't matched. Add it by hand or call them back.
topics.tip: This box is the one thing to check before confirming a WhatsApp order. Without it the draft looks complete when half the request is missing.
topics.keywords: unmatched items missing amber whatsapp incomplete parse failed

topics.heading: Known customers are recognised
topics.body: If the number that messaged is already in your customer list, the order is attached to them automatically and shows the name you know them by — not whatever they've set on their phone. Formatting differences don't matter.
topics.tip: It only ever links existing customers. A stranger messaging once won't be added to your list.
topics.keywords: customer link phone number match recognise crm history

topics.heading: You're told when one arrives
topics.body: A card appears in the corner of whatever screen you're on when an order comes in from outside the shop, with a chime if sound is enabled in Settings. It flags orders that have unmatched items.
topics.tip: It only announces orders that arrive while you're working — opening the app doesn't replay everything already waiting.
topics.keywords: alert notification sound chime arrive incoming new order

topics.heading: Deliveries close with the receipt
topics.body: An order that goes out for delivery can't just be marked done. The printed receipt carries a code, and completing the order means scanning or typing that code back in — the proof that the slip came back with the money.
topics.tip: Orders collected in the shop don't need this. It exists for the case where cash travels with someone else.
topics.keywords: delivery receipt code scan barcode complete confirm proof

topics.heading: Who can do what
topics.body: Viewer sees but changes nothing. Cashier takes orders. Manager adds stock, moves orders, handles cash. Admin manages people too. Individual permissions can be granted on top of a role, and every one is enforced by the server — not just hidden on screen.
topics.tip: For a genuinely read-only account use the Viewer role. Unticking a cashier's boxes doesn't achieve it, because permissions only ever add.
topics.keywords: roles permissions cashier manager admin viewer capabilities access

topics.heading: Accounts are created by invitation
topics.body: There's no public signup. An admin invites an email address and the person sets their own password from the link — so nobody, including you, ever types someone else's password.
topics.tip: If the invite email doesn't arrive, the link is shown on screen when you create the invite. Copy it and send it any way you like.
topics.keywords: invite account signup password email link new user staff

topics.heading: Ban rather than delete
topics.body: Banning blocks sign-in immediately and keeps the person's history intact. Deleting is refused for anyone who has ever touched an order, stock or cash — those records must stay attributable to whoever made them.
topics.keywords: ban delete disable user leaver history audit attributable

topics.heading: Making the receipt yours
topics.body: Settings controls the roll width, what appears on the slip, your shop's name, phone and address, and your logo. You can upload an image straight from your phone or computer — no image hosting needed.
topics.tip: There are two logos: one for the printed receipt and one for the app. A thermal printer wants a simple high-contrast mark; the screen can take the full-colour one.
topics.keywords: receipt customize logo width printer settings header footer branding

topics.heading: The numbers look wrong
topics.body: Check the obvious three first: is the order still a draft (nothing deducted), was stock edited by hand (the reason is recorded on the product), and are you comparing cash position against revenue when they measure different things.
topics.keywords: wrong numbers mismatch stock cash difference investigate

topics.heading: A page says you have no access
topics.body: Every module is visible to everyone; the page itself refuses if your role lacks the permission. If you think that's wrong, an admin can grant the specific capability without changing your whole role.
topics.tip: A brief spinner is not a refusal. If it settles into a refusal message, that's the real answer.
topics.keywords: no access denied permission forbidden role capability

topics.heading: Nothing is loading
topics.body: If the app says it can't reach the server, that's the connection, not your data — nothing has been lost and nothing was half-saved. Check the shop's internet and try again.
topics.tip: Error messages that name a specific thing ("that code belongs to a different order") are the app telling you something real. "Can't reach the server" is the network.
topics.keywords: offline network error connection loading failed server down

topics.heading: Scanning scale labels
topics.body: If your scale prints its own labels, scanning one adds the product at its exact weight — no keying the kg. Set the label layout once in Settings → Scale barcodes, and give each product its scale item code in Inventory. Ordinary product barcodes still scan as before.
topics.tip: Use the tester in Settings with a real label before trusting it. A wrong digit position reads the wrong weight or the wrong product, and the tester shows both.
topics.keywords: scale label weight embedded plu variable measure barcode scan ean

topics.heading: Setting up outgoing email
topics.body: Invite and password-reset emails go out one of two ways. If you've connected an automation (Make or similar) to the webhook, they send through it — from your linked Google account, which is what makes them arrive reliably. If no automation is connected, they fall back to Brevo, set up under Settings → Email fallback: a verified sender address, a sender name, and a Brevo API key. Each field there carries an ⓘ with the details.
topics.tip: Never use a free @gmail.com address as the Brevo sender — mail 'from' a gmail address sent through Brevo fails authentication and is treated as spam. Use an address on a domain you own and verify it in Brevo. On the Make path that field is only the reply-to, so a gmail address there is harmless.
topics.keywords: email invite password reset brevo make sender api key smtp spam dmarc reply-to setup configure

topics.heading: Setting up scale barcodes
topics.body: Under Settings → Scale barcodes, describe your scale's label layout: the prefix that marks a scale label, the total length, and which digit positions hold the item code and the value. Choose whether the value is a weight or a price, and the divisor (weight in grams → 1000, price with 2 decimals → 100). Then give each product its scale item code in Inventory. A scan on New Order adds the item at its exact weight.
topics.tip: Don't count digit positions by eye — paste a real printed label into the tester on that screen. It shows the item code, the weight or price it read, and which product it matched, so a wrong position is obvious before it reaches the counter.
topics.keywords: scale setup configure prefix position item code weight price divisor tester plu inventory ean

topics.heading: What a scale PLU is
topics.body: A PLU (Price Look-Up) is the short number your scale uses to identify a product — it is not the shelf barcode. Each product is programmed on the scale with a PLU, a name and a price per kilo. The scale bakes that PLU into the label's barcode, which is how the app knows which product you weighed. Put the same number in that product's "Scale item code" in Inventory — that field is the link between the two systems.
topics.tip: Copy the item code exactly as the tester shows it, leading zeros and all: the match is character-for-character, so 00021 and 21 are not the same thing. And never reuse the shelf barcode as a PLU — they're different numbers.
topics.keywords: plu price look up scale item code product match inventory link plu list programming

topics.heading: Working out your scale's barcode layout
topics.body: Print a label for a product whose PLU and weight (or price) you already know. Write the barcode's digits out and number them from 1. Now hunt for the number you already know — a 1.234 kg weight appears as 1234 — and where it starts and how many digits it spans are your value start and length. Do the same for the PLU to get the item code start and length. The divisor puts the decimal point back: grams → 1000, price with two decimals → 100.
topics.tip: Don't work from a sample label in a brochure. Demo labels almost always carry an all-zero item code and invented figures, so they can't show you where the PLU sits — only a real label off your own scale can.
topics.keywords: layout positions digits find work out prefix divisor sample demo label decode map

topics.heading: Weight labels vs price labels
topics.body: A scale can embed either the weight or the total price in its barcode. Weight is the better choice: the app prices it from the product's own rate. With price, the app has to work the weight back out as price ÷ price-per-kg, so if the scale's rate and the app's ever drift apart, the weight it records is wrong.
topics.tip: If your scale can print weight-embedded labels, pick Weight and set the divisor to 1000 (grams). Only choose Price if the scale can't do weight — and then keep the scale's price-per-kg and the app's in step.
topics.keywords: weight price embedded mode divisor grams decimals choose which

settings_page.subtitle: Shop-wide alerts, stock, and email preferences
settings_page.no_access: You don't have access to shop settings.
settings_page.error_minutes: Enter a pending-order alert threshold greater than 0.
settings_page.error_threshold: Enter a low-stock threshold greater than 0.
settings_page.section_email_note: Used only when no automation webhook is configured. With Make connected, invite and password-reset emails send through it — and of the fields below, only the sender email is used, as the reply-to address. Sender name and the Brevo key apply only on this Brevo fallback.
settings_page.pending_order_minutes_hint: How long a draft order can sit unconfirmed before it's flagged as stale on the dashboard.
settings_page.alert_sound_label: Play a sound for new alerts
settings_page.low_stock_threshold_hint: Used for any product without its own threshold set in Inventory.
settings_page.sender_name_hint: The "From" display name on the Brevo fallback path. When emails go through Make, the display name is your connected Google account’s name instead, and this is ignored.
settings_page.brevo_sender_hint: Your shop's contact address. When emails go through Make it's used as the reply-to (the message itself is sent from your connected Google account, which is what makes it deliver reliably). On the Brevo fallback it's the verified From — add and verify it at app.brevo.com under Senders. Avoid a free @gmail.com address here for the Brevo path: it fails authentication and lands in spam.
settings_page.brevo_api_key_hint_configured: A key is already saved. Type a new one and click Update to replace it, or leave this blank — Update won't clear it.
settings_page.brevo_api_key_hint_unconfigured: From app.brevo.com under SMTP & API → API Keys. Free tier covers 300 emails/day.

receipt.title: Receipt
receipt.hint: Controls how the printed receipt looks. Changes apply to the next receipt printed.
receipt.delivery_fee_hint: Added to the total of any order with a delivery address, and printed on the receipt as its own line. Counter sales are unaffected. Changing it never alters orders already taken.

settings_page.delivery_name_hint: What your shop calls whoever takes an order out — Driver, Courier, Delivery. Shown on the order board and in order details, never printed on the receipt.

logo.app_hint: Shown in the navigation rail. Leave empty to use the default mark.
logo.receipt_hint: Printed at the top of the receipt. A high-contrast image works best on a thermal printer.
logo.uploaded_placeholder: Uploaded image — remove it to paste a URL instead
logo.too_large: Image is still over {{kb}} KB after resizing. Try a simpler or smaller image.
logo.unreadable: That file couldn't be read as an image.
logo.file_too_large: File is over {{mb}} MB. Pick a smaller one.
logo.too_small: Image is smaller than {{px}}×{{px}} px — it would look blurry.
logo.limits: PNG, JPG, WebP or SVG · up to {{mb}} MB · at least {{px}}px · resized automatically

settings_page.scale_section_note: If your weighing scale prints its own labels, describe their layout here and a scan on the New Order screen will add the exact weight. Positions are counted from the first digit. Not sure of the layout? Fill in your best guess and use the tester below with a real label.
settings_page.scale_divisor_hint: Divide the value digits by this. Weight in grams → 1000 (12340 → 12.340 kg). Price with 2 decimals → 100 (04599 → 45.99).
settings_page.scale_test_placeholder: Paste a barcode from a printed label
settings_page.scale_test_no_match: Not read as a scale label — check the prefix and total length.
settings_page.scale_test_no_product: No product has this item code yet — add it in Inventory.

admin_users_page.title: User Management
admin_users_page.subtitle: Manage roles and permissions
admin_users_page.invite_email_failed: Couldn't send the email to {{email}} — share this link with them directly instead.
admin_users_page.confirm_demote_message: You're changing your own role away from Admin. You'll lose access to this screen until another admin restores it.
admin_users_page.cannot_self: You can't ban or delete your own account.
admin_users_page.reset_link_hint: Generate a password-reset link for this user and email it. The link is also shown here so you can hand it over directly if email doesn't arrive.
admin_users_page.reset_link_sent: Emailed — and here's the link in case it doesn't arrive:
admin_users_page.reset_link_not_sent: The email didn't send. Share this link with them directly:
admin_users_page.reset_link_expiry: This link expires in 1 hour and replaces any previous one.
admin_users_page.confirm_delete_message: This cannot be undone. Accounts with order, stock or cash history can't be deleted — ban them instead, which blocks sign-in immediately and keeps the audit trail.

customers_page.title: Customers
customers_page.delete_confirm: This permanently erases their record. Are you sure?

inbox_page.title: Order Inbox
inbox_page.parse_hint: Reads the pasted text and adds matching products to the cart automatically — review before saving.
inbox_page.parse_unmatched: Added the matching items. Couldn't confidently match: {{items}} — add those by hand.
inbox_page.parse_no_matches: Couldn't confidently match any items in that message — add them by hand below.

cash_page.title: Cash Management
cash_page.no_access: You don't have permission to view cash management.
cash_page.confirm_close_message: This resets order numbering back to #1 and records a permanent snapshot of orders, revenue, and cash since the last closing. It doesn't affect the cash ledger itself — every entry stays in the table below.

errors.NETWORK: Can't reach the server. Check the connection and try again.
errors.UNAUTHORIZED: You need to sign in to do that.
errors.CREDENTIALS_REQUIRED: Enter both an email and a password.
errors.ACCOUNT_BANNED: This account has been disabled. Contact an administrator.
errors.TOKEN_INVALID_OR_EXPIRED: This link is invalid or has expired. Request a new one.
errors.TOO_MANY_LOGIN_ATTEMPTS: Too many sign-in attempts. Wait a moment and try again.
errors.TOO_MANY_REQUESTS: Too many requests. Wait a moment and try again.
errors.ROLE_REQUIRED: This needs the {{role}} role or higher.
errors.CAP_REQUIRED: You don't have permission for this ({{cap}}).
errors.VALIDATION_FAILED: Some fields aren't filled in correctly.
errors.DISMANTLE_EVENT_NOT_FOUND: That dismantle record no longer exists.
errors.ORDER_NOT_DRAFT_PROMOTE: Only drafts can be confirmed. This order is already confirmed.
errors.ORDER_NOT_DRAFT_EDIT: Only drafts can be edited. This order has already moved stock — cancel it instead.
errors.ORDER_NOT_DRAFT_DELETE: Only drafts can be deleted. Cancel a confirmed order instead.
errors.STATUS_NOT_ALLOWED: This order can't move to that status from here.
errors.INSUFFICIENT_STOCK: Not enough {{name}} in stock. Available: {{available}} kg.
errors.RECEIPT_SCAN_WRONG_STATUS: Only on-the-way orders are confirmed by scanning a receipt.
errors.RECEIPT_CODE_MISMATCH: That code belongs to a different order. Check the receipt.
errors.EMAIL_ALREADY_EXISTS: That email already has an account. Email addresses are unique across every shop, so use a different one.
errors.LAST_ACTIVE_ADMIN: This is the only active admin — make someone else an admin first.
errors.CANNOT_TARGET_SELF: You can't ban or delete your own account.
errors.USER_HAS_HISTORY: This user has order, stock or cash history that must stay attributable. Ban the account instead.
errors.ENCRYPTION_KEY_MISSING: The server isn't set up to store API keys yet. Ask whoever deploys the app to set SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY.
errors.SERVER_ERROR: Something went wrong on the server. Try again.
errors.DISMANTLE_OUTPUT_AMBIGUOUS: "{{name}}" links to both an existing product and a new one — pick one.
errors.DISMANTLE_OUTPUT_FOREIGN: That output doesn't belong to this dismantle record.
errors.ORGANIZATION_ARCHIVED: This account has been closed. Contact support.
errors.WRONG_ORGANIZATION: You're signed in to a different shop. Sign out and sign in again on this one.
errors.BILLING_SUSPENDED: This account is read-only while billing is suspended. Contact support.
errors.SUPER_ADMIN_REQUIRED: That's restricted to the operator of this deployment.
errors.SLUG_INVALID: Subdomain must be 3-40 characters of lowercase letters, digits or hyphens, and not a reserved name.
errors.SLUG_IMMUTABLE: A subdomain can't be changed — it's in every bookmark and invite link already sent.
errors.ORGANIZATION_HAS_DATA: This organization has users or trading history. Archive it instead of deleting it.
errors.SCALE_ITEM_NOT_FOUND: No product matches that scale item code. Add the code in Inventory.
errors.SCALE_PRICE_UNRESOLVABLE: This product has no price per kg, so a weight can't be worked out from the label's price.

public.badge: Built for butcher shops · Arabic & English
public.subhead: A cashier system for shops that sell by weight. Take orders from the counter, phone or WhatsApp, watch them move to the customer, and close the day knowing the stock and the till agree.
public.highlight_orders: One board from order taken to order delivered
public.highlight_stock: Stock falls as you sell — to three decimal places
public.highlight_cash: Every cash sale logged the moment it happens
public.cta_note: Accounts are created by your shop's admin — there's no public signup.
public.features_title: Built around how a butcher shop actually runs
public.feature_orders_body: Created, in progress, on the way, in the shop, done. Longest-waiting order at the top of each column, with a clock on every card.
public.feature_weight_body: Products are kept in kilograms to three decimals, priced per kilo. No pretending meat comes in whole units.
public.feature_cash_body: Cash sales post to the ledger automatically. Log what goes in and out by hand, then close the day against a permanent snapshot.
public.feature_dismantle_body: Break a calf, sheep or goat into cuts against a template, and have the resulting stock land in inventory with the yield recorded.
public.feature_receipts_body: Set the roll width, what's printed and your own logo. Every slip carries a barcode that confirms a delivery when it comes back.
public.feature_roles_body: Cashier, manager, admin — or view-only. Permissions are enforced on the server, not just hidden in the screen.
public.step_take_body: At the counter, over the phone, or parsed straight from a WhatsApp message into a draft you confirm.
public.step_track_body: The board shows what's cut, what's out for delivery and how long it's been. Late orders surface on the dashboard by themselves.
public.step_close_body: One snapshot of the day's orders and cash, the order numbers reset, and the ledger stays intact behind it.
public.footer_note: A cashier system for shops that sell by weight

organizations_page.title: Organizations
organizations_page.subtitle: Every shop on this deployment. Super admin only.
organizations_page.no_access: This screen is for the operator of this deployment, not for shop accounts.

organizations_page.invite_not_sent: Organization created, but the invite email didn't send. Share this link directly.
organizations_page.search_placeholder: Search by name, subdomain or email…
organizations_page.suspended_note: Suspended: this shop can still read its records but cannot create or change anything.

idle.title: Still there?
idle.body: You'll be signed out shortly for security.
idle.signed_out: You were signed out after a period of inactivity.
